fields.summary: Define an in-memory implementation of ApplicationTimelineStore
[Umbrella] Store, manage and serve per-framework application-timeline data

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

issuetype.description: A problem which impairs or prevents the functions of the product.
issuetype.name: Bug

fields.description: As per the design doc, the store needs to pluggable. We need a base interface, and an in-memory implementation for testing.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

fields.summary: Define the ApplicationTimelineStore store as an abstraction for implementing different storage impls for storing timeline information

status.description: The issue is considered finished, the resolution is correct. Issues which are not closed can be reopened.
status.name: Closed

fields.summary: Implement timeline related web-services inside AHS for storing and retrieving entities+events

status.description: The issue is considered finished, the resolution is correct. Issues which are not closed can be reopened.
status.name: Closed

comments.body: Upload a patch of in-memory implementation of ApplicationTimelineStore wit the test cases available.

{color:red}-1 overall{color}.  Here are the results of testing the latest attachment 
  http://issues.apache.org/jira/secure/attachment/12626156/YARN-1634.1.patch
  against trunk revision .

    {color:green}+1 @author{color}.  The patch does not contain any @author tags.

    {color:green}+1 tests included{color}.  The patch appears to include 2 new or modified test files.

    {color:red}-1 javac{color:red}.  The patch appears to cause the build to fail.

This message is automatically generated.

Looks great overall. Some comments on the patch
 - Make ApplicationTimelineStoreUtils.test() protected
 - The test is good but very long. Can you further split test() method into smaller methods?
 - Add a little documentation for MemoryApplicationTimelineStore.maskFields()
 - Similar documentation for ApplicationTimelineStoreTestUtils methods.

There's a lot of boiler plate in creating events/entities etc. Will file a separate JIRA for utilities.

Please fix the RAT warning. Javadoc is unrelated?

Upload a new patch:

1. Refactor the test code
2. Fix RAT warning

The javadoc warnings should be unrelated.
